sanskrit:
तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व जित्वा शत्रून् भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम्।
मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन्॥
transliteration: tasmāt tvam uttiṣṭha yaśo labhasva jitvā śatrūn bhuṅkṣva rājyaṃ samṛddham | mayaivaite nihatāḥ pūrvameva nimittamātraṃ bhava savyasācin ||
literal_meaning: Therefore you arise, gain fame, having conquered the enemies enjoy the flourishing kingdom; by Me alone these have already been slain long ago—become just the instrument, O Savyasācin.
contextual_meaning: Krishna dissolves Arjuna’s egoic ownership of action: the battle is already resolved on a higher plane; Arjuna’s task is to drop resistance, surrender doer-identity, and allow the Self to act through him without personal claim on outcome.
psychological_state: Post-visionary ego surrender: the self-image as autonomous agent collapses, replaced by felt participation in a larger order; healthy dissociation from omnipotent fantasy and crushing responsibility.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon who must operate on a loved one realizes the outcome is beyond personal control; she scrubs in, trusting training and team, and acts as a calm conduit rather than a self-saving hero.
anchor_text: Step aside and let life fight through you.
